assertion_results.text: The plan explicitly references reusing common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter for comma-separated multi-value query parameter parsing rather than writing new parsing logic (constraint 5.4)
assertion_results.evidence: plan.md line 23-24: 'Use `common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter` to parse the comma-separated license string and generate the appropriate SQL IN clause. This function already handles both single and multi-value comma-separated parameters, so no new parsing logic is needed.' Also referenced in the Data Flow Trace at line 88: '`apply_filter` from `common/src/db/query.rs` splits the comma-separated string into individual values.'

assertion_results.text: The plan references following the filter pattern from modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs as a structural guide for implementing the license filter

Also line 26-27: 'The `severity` filter in `modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs` demonstrates the exact same pattern -- an optional query parameter field parsed with `apply_filter` and passed to the service layer.' assertion_results.evidence: plan.md line 33-34: 'add a JOIN to the `package_license` table (using the `entity/src/package_license.rs` SeaORM entity) and a WHERE clause filtering on the license SPDX identifier column.' reuse-analysis.md lines 41-43: 'Use the `package_license` entity in the `PackageService::list` method to construct a JOIN query... Use SeaORM's relation-based join.' reuse-analysis.md line 47: 'Without using the existing entity, the implementation would need to write raw SQL JOINs or define a new entity/relation for the same table.'

assertion_results.text: The reuse analysis covers all 3 Reuse Candidates from the task description, explaining how each would be applied
assertion_results.evidence: reuse-analysis.md has three dedicated sections: 'Reuse Candidate 1: `common/src/db/query.rs::apply_filter`' (lines 9-17) with 'How it will be reused' explanation; 'Reuse Candidate 2: `modules/fundamental/src/advisory/endpoints/list.rs`' (lines 19-33) with detailed three-point 'How it will be reused' explanation covering Query struct pattern, Handler pattern, and Service integration pattern; 'Reuse Candidate 3: `entity/src/package_license.rs`' (lines 35-48) with 'How it will be reused' explanation covering JOIN construction and Filter application.

The plan proposes calling apply_filter directly. reuse-analysis.md line 16 confirms: 'Reuse type: Direct invocation -- no modification or extension of `apply_filter` is needed.'
